tEn: How Does Batch Normalization Help Optimization?
tZh: 批归一化如何帮助优化？
sEn: Batch Normalization makes the optimization landscape smoother, enabling faster and more stable training.
sZh: 批归一化使优化景观更平滑，从而实现更快、更稳定的训练。
absEn: Batch Normalization (BatchNorm) is a widely adopted technique that enables faster and more stable training of deep neural networks (DNNs). Despite its pervasiveness, the exact reasons for BatchNorm's effectiveness are still poorly understood. The popular belief is that this effectiveness stems from controlling the change of the layers' input distributions during training to reduce the so-called "internal covariate shift". In this work, we demonstrate that such distributional stability of layer inputs has little to do with the success of BatchNorm. Instead, we uncover a more fundamental impact of BatchNorm on the training process: it makes the optimization landscape significantly smoother. This smoothness induces a more predictive and stable behavior of the gradients, allowing for faster training.
absZh: 批归一化（BatchNorm）是一种广泛采用的技术，能够加速深度神经网络的训练并使其更稳定。尽管其普遍应用，但BatchNorm有效性的确切原因仍不清楚。普遍认为其有效性源于控制训练过程中层输入分布的变化，以减少所谓的“内部协变量偏移”。在本工作中，我们证明这种层输入的分布稳定性与BatchNorm的成功关系不大。相反，我们揭示了BatchNorm对训练过程更根本的影响：它使优化景观显著更平滑。这种平滑性导致梯度更具可预测性和稳定性，从而允许更快的训练。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that BatchNorm's success is not due to reducing internal covariate shift.
contrib.zh: 证明BatchNorm的成功并非源于减少内部协变量偏移。

contrib.en: Identifies that BatchNorm smooths the optimization landscape, improving gradient predictiveness.
contrib.zh: 发现BatchNorm平滑优化曲面，提升梯度的可预测性。

contrib.en: Shows that BatchNorm improves Lipschitzness of loss and gradients, enabling larger learning rates.
contrib.zh: 表明BatchNorm改善损失和梯度的Lipschitz连续性，允许更大学习率。

contrib.en: Provides theoretical analysis proving BatchNorm reduces gradient magnitude and Hessian quadratic form.
contrib.zh: 提供理论分析证明BatchNorm降低梯度幅值和Hessian二次型。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that other normalization techniques (e.g., Lp-norm) also smooth the landscape.
contrib.zh: 证明其他归一化技术（如Lp范数）同样平滑优化曲面。

limits.en: Analysis focuses on fully-connected layers; effects on convolutional layers may differ.
limits.zh: 分析聚焦全连接层；对卷积层的影响可能不同。

limits.en: Theoretical results assume mild conditions like convex loss and positive gradient-variable correlation.
limits.zh: 理论结果假设温和条件，如凸损失和梯度-变量正相关。

limits.en: Empirical validation limited to VGG on CIFAR-10 and deep linear networks.
limits.zh: 实证验证限于CIFAR-10上的VGG和深度线性网络。

limits.en: Does not fully explain BatchNorm's generalization benefits.
limits.zh: 未完全解释BatchNorm的泛化优势。

items.en: Batch Normalization (BatchNorm) is a widely adopted technique that enables faster and more stable training of deep neural networks (DNNs). Despite its pervasiveness, the exact reasons for BatchNorm’s effectiveness are still poorly understood. The popular belief is that this effectiveness stems from controlling the change of the layers’ input distributions during training to reduce the so-called “internal covariate shift”. In this work, we demonstrate that such distributional stability of layer inputs has little to do with the success of BatchNorm. Instead, we uncover a more fundamental impact of BatchNorm on the training process: it makes the optimization landscape significantly smoother. This smoothness induces a more predictive and stable behavior of the gradients, allowing for faster training.
items.zh: 批量归一化（BatchNorm）是一种被广泛采用的技术，能够实现深度神经网络（DNN）更快、更稳定的训练。尽管其普遍存在，但BatchNorm有效性的确切原因仍未被充分理解。普遍认为，这种有效性源于控制训练过程中层输入分布的变化，以减少所谓的“内部协变量偏移”。在本工作中，我们证明这种层输入的分布稳定性与BatchNorm的成功关系不大。相反，我们揭示了BatchNorm对训练过程更根本的影响：它使优化景观显著更平滑。这种平滑性导致梯度更具可预测性和稳定性，从而实现更快的训练。

items.en: Over the last decade, deep learning has made impressive progress on a variety of notoriously difficult tasks in computer vision [16, 7], speech recognition [5], machine translation [29], and game-playing [18, 25]. This progress hinged on a number of major advances in terms of hardware, datasets [15, 23], and algorithmic and architectural techniques [27, 12, 20, 28]. One of the most prominent examples of such advances was batch normalization (BatchNorm) [10].
items.zh: 在过去十年中，深度学习在计算机视觉[16, 7]、语音识别[5]、机器翻译[29]和游戏[18, 25]等众多公认困难的任务上取得了令人瞩目的进展。这一进展依赖于硬件、数据集[15, 23]以及算法和架构技术[27, 12, 20, 28]方面的多项重大突破。其中最具代表性的进展之一是批量归一化（BatchNorm）[10]。

items.en: At a high level, BatchNorm is a technique that aims to improve the training of neural networks by stabilizing the distributions of layer inputs. This is achieved by introducing additional network layers that control the first two moments (mean and variance) of these distributions.
items.zh: 从高层次来看，BatchNorm是一种旨在通过稳定层输入分布来改进神经网络训练的技术。这是通过引入额外的网络层来控制这些分布的前两个矩（均值和方差）来实现的。

items.en: The practical success of BatchNorm is indisputable. By now, it is used by default in most deep learning models, both in research (more than 6,000 citations) and real-world settings. Somewhat shockingly, however, despite its prominence, we still have a poor understanding of what the effectiveness of BatchNorm is stemming from. In fact, there are now a number of works that provide alternatives to BatchNorm [1, 3, 13, 31], but none of them seem to bring us any closer to understanding this issue. (A similar point was also raised recently in [22].)
items.zh: BatchNorm的实际成功是无可争议的。如今，它在大多数深度学习模型中默认使用，无论是在研究领域（超过6000次引用）还是实际应用中。然而，令人震惊的是，尽管其重要性，我们对其有效性的根源仍然知之甚少。事实上，现在有许多工作提供了BatchNorm的替代方案[1, 3, 13, 31]，但似乎没有一个能让我们更接近理解这个问题。（类似的观点最近也在[22]中提出。）

items.en: Currently, the most widely accepted explanation of BatchNorm’s success, as well as its original motivation, relates to so-called internal covariate shift (ICS). Informally, ICS refers to the change in the distribution of layer inputs caused by updates to the preceding layers. It is conjectured that such continual change negatively impacts training. The goal of BatchNorm was to reduce ICS and thus remedy this effect.
items.zh: 目前，对BatchNorm成功最广为接受的解释及其最初动机，与所谓的内部协变量偏移（ICS）有关。非正式地说，ICS指的是由于前一层参数的更新而导致层输入分布的变化。据推测，这种持续变化会对训练产生负面影响。BatchNorm的目标是减少ICS，从而改善这种影响。

items.en: Even though this explanation is widely accepted, we seem to have little concrete evidence supporting it. In particular, we still do not understand the link between ICS and training performance. The chief goal of this paper is to address all these shortcomings. Our exploration lead to somewhat startling discoveries.
items.zh: 尽管这一解释被广泛接受，但我们似乎缺乏具体的证据来支持它。特别是，我们仍然不了解ICS与训练性能之间的联系。本文的主要目标是解决所有这些不足。我们的探索带来了一些令人惊讶的发现。

items.en: Our point of start is demonstrating that there does not seem to be any link between the performance gain of BatchNorm and the reduction of internal covariate shift. Or that this link is tenuous, at best. In fact, we find that in a certain sense BatchNorm might not even be reducing internal covariate shift.
items.zh: 我们的出发点是证明BatchNorm的性能提升与内部协变量偏移的减少之间似乎没有任何联系。或者说，这种联系充其量是微弱的。事实上，我们发现从某种意义上说，BatchNorm甚至可能没有减少内部协变量偏移。

items.en: We then turn our attention to identifying the roots of BatchNorm’s success. Specifically, we demonstrate that BatchNorm impacts network training in a fundamental way: it makes the landscape of the corresponding optimization problem significantly more smooth. This ensures, in particular, that the gradients are more predictive and thus allows for use of larger range of learning rates and faster network convergence. We provide an empirical demonstration of these findings as well as their theoretical justification. We prove that, under natural conditions, the Lipschitzness of both the loss and the gradients (also known as β𝛽\beta-smoothness [21]) are improved in models with BatchNorm.
items.zh: 然后，我们将注意力转向识别BatchNorm成功的根源。具体来说，我们证明BatchNorm以一种根本性的方式影响网络训练：它使相应优化问题的景观显著更平滑。这尤其确保了梯度更具可预测性，从而允许使用更大范围的学习率和更快的网络收敛。我们提供了这些发现的实证证明以及理论依据。我们证明，在自然条件下，损失和梯度的Lipschitz连续性（也称为β-平滑性[21]）在具有BatchNorm的模型中得到了改善。

items.en: Finally, we find that this smoothening effect is not uniquely tied to BatchNorm. A number of other natural normalization techniques have a similar (and, sometime, even stronger) effect. In particular, they all offer similar improvements in the training performance.
items.zh: 最后，我们发现这种平滑效应并非BatchNorm所独有。许多其他自然归一化技术具有类似（有时甚至更强）的效果。特别是，它们都在训练性能上提供了类似的改进。

items.en: We believe that understanding the roots of such a fundamental techniques as BatchNorm will let us have a significantly better grasp of the underlying complexities of neural network training and, in turn, will inform further algorithmic progress in this context.
items.zh: 我们相信，理解像BatchNorm这样基本技术的根源，将使我们能够更好地把握神经网络训练的潜在复杂性，进而为这一背景下的进一步算法进展提供信息。

items.en: Our paper is organized as follows. In Section 2, we explore the connections between BatchNorm, optimization, and internal covariate shift. Then, in Section 3, we demonstrate and analyze the exact roots of BatchNorm’s success in deep neural network training. We present our theoretical analysis in Section 4. We discuss further related work in Section 5 and conclude in Section 6.
items.zh: 本文的组织结构如下。在第2节中，我们探讨BatchNorm、优化和内部协变量偏移之间的联系。然后，在第3节中，我们展示并分析BatchNorm在深度神经网络训练中成功的确切根源。我们在第4节中提出理论分析。我们在第5节中讨论进一步的相关工作，并在第6节中总结。

full.sec: 2 Batch normalization and internal covariate shift
full.secZh: 2 批归一化与内部协变量偏移

items.en: Batch normalization (BatchNorm) [10] has been arguably one of the most successful architectural innovations in deep learning. But even though its effectiveness is indisputable, we do not have a firm understanding of why this is the case.
items.zh: 批量归一化（BatchNorm）[10]可以说是深度学习中最成功的架构创新之一。但尽管其有效性无可争议，我们对其原因并没有坚实的理解。

items.en: Broadly speaking, BatchNorm is a mechanism that aims to stabilize the distribution (over a mini-batch) of inputs to a given network layer during training. This is achieved by augmenting the network with additional layers that set the first two moments (mean and variance) of the distribution of each activation to be zero and one respectively. Then, the batch normalized inputs are also typically scaled and shifted based on trainable parameters to preserve model expressivity. This normalization is applied before the non-linearity of the previous layer.
items.zh: 广义上讲，BatchNorm是一种旨在训练过程中稳定给定网络层输入（在小批量上）分布的机制。这是通过向网络添加额外的层来实现的，这些层将每个激活分布的前两个矩（均值和方差）分别设置为0和1。然后，批量归一化的输入通常还会根据可训练参数进行缩放和平移，以保持模型表达能力。这种归一化在前一层的非线性之前应用。

items.en: One of the key motivations for the development of BatchNorm was the reduction of so-called internal covariate shift (ICS). This reduction has been widely viewed as the root of BatchNorm’s success. Ioffe and Szegedy [10] describe ICS as the phenomenon wherein the distribution of inputs to a layer in the network changes due to an update of parameters of the previous layers. This change leads to a constant shift of the underlying training problem and is thus believed to have detrimental effect on the training process.
items.zh: 开发BatchNorm的主要动机之一是减少所谓的内部协变量偏移（ICS）。这种减少被广泛认为是BatchNorm成功的根源。Ioffe和Szegedy [10]将ICS描述为由于前一层参数更新而导致网络层输入分布发生变化的现象。这种变化导致底层训练问题的持续偏移，因此被认为对训练过程有不利影响。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Comparison of (a) training (optimization) and (b) test (generalization) performance of a standard VGG network trained on CIFAR-10 with and without BatchNorm (details in Appendix A). There is a consistent gain in training speed in models with BatchNorm layers. (c) Even though the gap between the performance of the BatchNorm and non-BatchNorm networks is clear, the difference in the evolution of layer input distributions seems to be much less pronounced. (Here, we sampled activations of a given layer and visualized their distribution over training steps.)
items.capZh: 图1：在CIFAR-10上训练的标准VGG网络（有无BatchNorm）的（a）训练（优化）和（b）测试（泛化）性能比较（详见附录A）。具有BatchNorm层的模型在训练速度上有一致的提升。（c）尽管BatchNorm网络和非BatchNorm网络之间的性能差距很明显，但层输入分布演变的差异似乎不那么显著。（这里，我们采样了给定层的激活，并可视化了它们在训练步骤中的分布。）

items.en: Despite its fundamental role and widespread use in deep learning, the underpinnings of BatchNorm’s success remain poorly understood [22]. In this work we aim to address this gap. To this end, we start by investigating the connection between ICS and BatchNorm. Specifically, we consider first training a standard VGG [26] architecture on CIFAR-10 [15] with and without BatchNorm. As expected, Figures 1(a) and (b) show a drastic improvement, both in terms of optimization and generalization performance, for networks trained with BatchNorm layers. Figure 1(c) presents, however, a surprising finding. In this figure, we visualize to what extent BatchNorm is stabilizing distributions of layer inputs by plotting the distribution (over a batch) of a random input over training. Surprisingly, the difference in distributional stability (change in the mean and variance) in networks with and without BatchNorm layers seems to be marginal. This observation raises the following questions:
items.zh: 尽管BatchNorm在深度学习中具有基础性作用并被广泛使用，但其成功的根本原因仍然未被充分理解[22]。在本工作中，我们旨在填补这一空白。为此，我们首先研究ICS与BatchNorm之间的联系。具体来说，我们考虑在CIFAR-10 [15]上训练标准VGG [26]架构，分别使用和不使用BatchNorm。正如预期，图1(a)和(b)显示，使用BatchNorm层训练的网络在优化和泛化性能上都有显著提升。然而，图1(c)展示了一个令人惊讶的发现。在该图中，我们通过绘制训练过程中随机输入（在一个批次上）的分布，可视化了BatchNorm在多大程度上稳定了层输入分布。令人惊讶的是，有和没有BatchNorm层的网络在分布稳定性（均值和方差的变化）上的差异似乎很小。这一观察提出了以下问题：

items.en: Is the effectiveness of BatchNorm indeed related to internal covariate shift?
items.zh: BatchNorm的有效性是否确实与内部协变量偏移有关？

items.en: Is BatchNorm’s stabilization of layer input distributions even effective in reducing ICS?
items.zh: BatchNorm对层输入分布的稳定是否甚至能有效减少ICS？

items.en: We now explore these questions in more depth.
items.zh: 我们现在更深入地探讨这些问题。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Connections between distributional stability and BatchNorm performance: We compare VGG networks trained without BatchNorm (Standard), with BatchNorm (Standard + BatchNorm) and with explicit “covariate shift” added to BatchNorm layers (Standard + “Noisy” BatchNorm). In the later case, we induce distributional instability by adding time-varying, non-zero mean and non-unit variance noise independently to each batch normalized activation. The “noisy” BatchNorm model nearly matches the performance of standard BatchNorm model, despite complete distributional instability. We sampled activations of a given layer and visualized their distributions (also cf. Figure 7).
items.capZh: 图2：分布稳定性与BatchNorm性能之间的联系：我们比较了没有BatchNorm（标准）、有BatchNorm（标准+BatchNorm）以及向BatchNorm层添加显式“协变量偏移”（标准+“噪声”BatchNorm）训练的VGG网络。在后一种情况下，我们通过向每个批量归一化激活独立添加时变、非零均值和非单位方差噪声来诱导分布不稳定性。“噪声”BatchNorm模型几乎匹配标准BatchNorm模型的性能，尽管分布完全不稳定。我们采样了给定层的激活并可视化了它们的分布（另见图7）。

full.sec: 2.1 Does BatchNorm’s performance stem from controlling internal covariate shift?
full.secZh: 2.1 批归一化的性能是否源于控制内部协变量偏移？

items.en: The central claim in [10] is that controlling the mean and variance of distributions of layer inputs is directly connected to improved training performance. Can we, however, substantiate this claim?
items.zh: [10]中的核心主张是，控制层输入分布的均值和方差与训练性能的提升直接相关。然而，我们能否证实这一主张？

items.en: We propose the following experiment. We train networks with random noise injected after BatchNorm layers. Specifically, we perturb each activation for each sample in the batch using i.i.d. noise sampled from a non-zero mean and non-unit variance distribution. We emphasize that this noise distribution changes at each time step (see Appendix A for implementation details).
items.zh: 我们提出以下实验。我们在BatchNorm层之后注入随机噪声来训练网络。具体来说，我们使用从非零均值和非单位方差分布中采样的独立同分布噪声，扰动批次中每个样本的每个激活值。我们强调，这种噪声分布在每个时间步都会发生变化（实现细节见附录A）。

items.en: Note that such noise injection produces a severe covariate shift that skews activations at every time step. Consequently, every unit in the layer experiences a different distribution of inputs at each time step. We then measure the effect of this deliberately introduced distributional instability on BatchNorm’s performance. Figure 2 visualizes the training behavior of standard, BatchNorm and our “noisy” BatchNorm networks. Distributions of activations over time from layers at the same depth in each one of the three networks are shown alongside.
items.zh: 注意，这种噪声注入会产生严重的协变量偏移，在每个时间步扭曲激活值。因此，该层中的每个单元在每个时间步都会经历不同的输入分布。然后，我们测量这种故意引入的分布不稳定性对BatchNorm性能的影响。图2可视化了标准网络、BatchNorm网络和我们的“噪声”BatchNorm网络的训练行为。同时展示了三个网络中相同深度层随时间变化的激活分布。

items.en: Observe that the performance difference between models with BatchNorm layers, and “noisy” BatchNorm layers is almost non-existent. Also, both these networks perform much better than standard networks. Moreover, the “noisy” BatchNorm network has qualitatively less stable distributions than even the standard, non-BatchNorm network, yet it still performs better in terms of training. To put the magnitude of the noise into perspective, we plot the mean and variance of random activations for select layers in Figure 7. Moreover, adding the same amount of noise to the activations of the standard (non-BatchNorm) network prevents it from training entirely.
items.zh: 观察到，带有BatchNorm层的模型与带有“噪声”BatchNorm层的模型之间的性能差异几乎不存在。此外，这两个网络的性能都远优于标准网络。而且，“噪声”BatchNorm网络的分布稳定性甚至比标准的非BatchNorm网络更差，但它在训练方面仍然表现更好。为了直观感受噪声的幅度，我们在图7中绘制了选定层的随机激活的均值和方差。此外，向标准（非BatchNorm）网络的激活中添加相同数量的噪声会使其完全无法训练。

items.en: Clearly, these findings are hard to reconcile with the claim that the performance gain due to BatchNorm stems from increased stability of layer input distributions.
items.zh: 显然，这些发现很难与BatchNorm带来的性能提升源于层输入分布稳定性增加的主张相协调。

full.sec: 2.2 Is BatchNorm reducing internal covariate shift?
full.secZh: 2.2 批归一化是否减少了内部协变量偏移？

items.en: Our findings in Section 2.1 make it apparent that ICS is not directly connected to the training performance, at least if we tie ICS to stability of the mean and variance of input distributions. One might wonder, however: Is there a broader notion of internal covariate shift that has such a direct link to training performance? And if so, does BatchNorm indeed reduce this notion?
items.zh: 我们在第2.1节中的发现表明，ICS与训练性能没有直接关联，至少如果我们把ICS与输入分布的均值和方差的稳定性联系起来的话。然而，人们可能会想：是否存在一个更广泛的内部协变量偏移概念，与训练性能有直接联系？如果存在，BatchNorm是否确实减少了这种偏移？

items.en: Recall that each layer can be seen as solving an empirical risk minimization problem where given a set of inputs, it is optimizing some loss function (that possibly involves later layers). An update to the parameters of any previous layer will change these inputs, thus changing this empirical risk minimization problem itself. This phenomenon is at the core of the intuition that Ioffe and Szegedy [10] provide regarding internal covariate shift. Specifically, they try to capture this phenomenon from the perspective of the resulting distributional changes in layer inputs. However, as demonstrated in Section 2.1, this perspective does not seem to properly encapsulate the roots of BatchNorm’s success.
items.zh: 回想一下，每一层都可以看作是在解决一个经验风险最小化问题，给定一组输入，它正在优化某个损失函数（可能涉及后续层）。对任何先前层参数的更新都会改变这些输入，从而改变这个经验风险最小化问题本身。这一现象是Ioffe和Szegedy [10]关于内部协变量偏移的直觉核心。具体来说，他们试图从层输入分布变化的角度来捕捉这一现象。然而，正如第2.1节所示，这一视角似乎并未正确概括BatchNorm成功的根源。

items.en: To answer this question, we consider a broader notion of internal covariate shift that is more tied to the underlying optimization task. (After all the success of BatchNorm is largely of an optimization nature.) Since the training procedure is a first-order method, the gradient of the loss is the most natural object to study. To quantify the extent to which the parameters in a layer would have to “adjust” in reaction to a parameter update in the previous layers, we measure the difference between the gradients of each layer before and after updates to all the previous layers. This leads to the following definition.
items.zh: 为了回答这个问题，我们考虑一个更广泛的内部协变量偏移概念，它与底层优化任务更相关。（毕竟BatchNorm的成功本质上是优化方面的。）由于训练过程是一阶方法，损失函数的梯度是最自然的研究对象。为了量化一层参数在响应前一层参数更新时需要“调整”的程度，我们测量了每一层在更新所有前层之前和之后的梯度差异。这引出了以下定义。

items.en: Let ℒℒ\mathcal{L} be the loss, W1(t)superscriptsubscript𝑊1𝑡W_{1}^{(t)}, …, Wk(t)superscriptsubscript𝑊𝑘𝑡W_{k}^{(t)} be the parameters of each of the k𝑘k layers and (x(t),y(t))superscript𝑥𝑡superscript𝑦𝑡(x^{(t)},y^{(t)}) be the batch of input-label pairs used to train the network at time t𝑡t. We define internal covariate shift (ICS) of activation i𝑖i at time t𝑡t to be the difference ‖Gt,i−Gt,i′‖2subscriptnormsubscript𝐺𝑡𝑖superscriptsubscript𝐺𝑡𝑖′2||G_{t,i}-G_{t,i}^{\prime}||_{2}, where
items.zh: 设ℒ为损失函数，W1(t), …, Wk(t)为k个层各自的参数，(x(t), y(t))为时间t用于训练网络的输入-标签对批次。我们将时间t时激活i的内部协变量偏移（ICS）定义为差‖Gt,i - Gt,i'‖2，其中

items.en: Here, Gt,isubscript𝐺𝑡𝑖G_{t,i} corresponds to the gradient of the layer parameters that would be applied during a simultaneous update of all layers (as is typical). On the other hand, Gt,i′subscriptsuperscript𝐺′𝑡𝑖G^{\prime}_{t,i} is the same gradient after all the previous layers have been updated with their new values. The difference between G𝐺G and G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime} thus reflects the change in the optimization landscape of Wisubscript𝑊𝑖W_{i} caused by the changes to its input. It thus captures precisely the effect of cross-layer dependencies that could be problematic for training.
items.zh: 这里，Gt,i对应于在同时更新所有层（典型情况）时应用的层参数的梯度。另一方面，Gt,i'是在所有前层已用新值更新后的相同梯度。因此，G与G'之间的差异反映了由输入变化引起的Wi优化景观的变化。它精确地捕捉了可能对训练有问题的跨层依赖效应。

items.en: Equipped with this definition, we measure the extent of ICS with and without BatchNorm layers. To isolate the effect of non-linearities as well as gradient stochasticity, we also perform this analysis on (25-layer) deep linear networks (DLN) trained with full-batch gradient descent (see Appendix A for details). The conventional understanding of BatchNorm suggests that the addition of BatchNorm layers in the network should increase the correlation between G𝐺G and G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}, thereby reducing ICS.
items.zh: 有了这个定义，我们测量了有无BatchNorm层时的ICS程度。为了隔离非线性和梯度随机性的影响，我们还在使用全批次梯度下降训练的（25层）深度线性网络（DLN）上进行了此分析（详见附录A）。对BatchNorm的传统理解表明，在网络中添加BatchNorm层应增加G与G'之间的相关性，从而减少ICS。

items.en: Surprisingly, we observe that networks with BatchNorm often exhibit an increase in ICS (cf. Figure 3). This is particularly striking in the case of DLN. In fact, in this case, the standard network experiences almost no ICS for the entirety of training, whereas for BatchNorm it appears that G𝐺G and G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime} are almost uncorrelated. We emphasize that this is the case even though BatchNorm networks continue to perform drastically better in terms of attained accuracy and loss. (The stabilization of the BatchNorm VGG network later in training is an artifact of faster convergence.) This evidence suggests that, from optimization point of view BatchNorm might not even reduce the internal covariate shift.
items.zh: 令人惊讶的是，我们观察到带有BatchNorm的网络通常表现出ICS的增加（见图3）。这在DLN的情况下尤为显著。事实上，在这种情况下，标准网络在整个训练过程中几乎没有经历ICS，而对于BatchNorm，G和G'几乎不相关。我们强调，即使BatchNorm网络在达到的准确率和损失方面持续表现更好，情况也是如此。（BatchNorm VGG网络在训练后期的稳定是更快收敛的产物。）这一证据表明，从优化的角度来看，BatchNorm可能甚至没有减少内部协变量偏移。

items.en: Our investigation so far demonstrated that the generally asserted link between the internal covariate shift (ICS) and the optimization performance is tenuous, at best. But BatchNorm does significantly improve the training process. Can we explain why this is the case?
items.zh: 我们迄今为止的研究表明，内部协变量偏移（ICS）与优化性能之间通常声称的联系充其量是脆弱的。但BatchNorm确实显著改善了训练过程。我们能解释这是为什么吗？

items.en: Aside from reducing ICS, Ioffe and Szegedy [10] identify a number of additional properties of BatchNorm. These include prevention of exploding or vanishing gradients, robustness to different settings of hyperparameters such as learning rate and initialization scheme, and keeping most of the activations away from saturation regions of non-linearities. All these properties are clearly beneficial to the training process. But they are fairly simple consequences of the mechanics of BatchNorm and do little to uncover the underlying factors responsible for BatchNorm’s success. Is there a more fundamental phenomenon at play here?
items.zh: 除了减少ICS，Ioffe和Szegedy [10]还指出了BatchNorm的一些其他属性。这些包括防止梯度爆炸或消失、对超参数（如学习率和初始化方案）不同设置的鲁棒性，以及使大多数激活远离非线性饱和区域。所有这些属性显然对训练过程有益。但它们只是BatchNorm机制的简单结果，并未揭示BatchNorm成功的根本因素。这里是否有更基本的现象在起作用？

items.en: Indeed, we identify the key impact that BatchNorm has on the training process: it reparametrizes the underlying optimization problem to make its landscape significantly more smooth. The first manifestation of this impact is improvement in the Lipschitzness111Recall that f𝑓f is L𝐿L-Lipschitz if |f​(x1)−f​(x2)|≤L​‖x1−x2‖𝑓subscript𝑥1𝑓subscript𝑥2𝐿normsubscript𝑥1subscript𝑥2|f(x_{1})-f(x_{2})|\leq L\|x_{1}-x_{2}\|, for all x1subscript𝑥1x_{1} and x2subscript𝑥2x_{2}. of the loss function. That is, the loss changes at a smaller rate and the magnitudes of the gradients are smaller too. There is, however, an even stronger effect at play. Namely, BatchNorm’s reparametrization makes gradients of the loss more Lipschitz too. In other words, the loss exhibits a significantly better “effective” β𝛽\beta-smoothness222Recall that f𝑓f is β𝛽\beta-smooth if its gradient is β𝛽\beta-Lipschitz. It is worth noting that, due to the existence of non-linearities, one should not expect the β𝛽\beta-smoothness to be bounded in an absolute, global sense..
items.zh: 确实，我们确定了BatchNorm对训练过程的关键影响：它重新参数化了底层优化问题，使其景观显著更平滑。这种影响的第一个表现是损失函数Lipschitz性的改善。也就是说，损失变化速率更小，梯度幅度也更小。然而，还有一个更强的效应在起作用。即，BatchNorm的重新参数化使损失函数的梯度更具Lipschitz性。换句话说，损失表现出显著更好的“有效”β-平滑性。

items.en: These smoothening effects impact the performance of the training algorithm in a major way. To understand why, recall that in a vanilla (non-BatchNorm), deep neural network, the loss function is not only non-convex but also tends to have a large number of “kinks”, flat regions, and sharp minima [17]. This makes gradient descent–based training algorithms unstable, e.g., due to exploding or vanishing gradients, and thus highly sensitive to the choice of the learning rate and initialization.
items.zh: 这些平滑效果对训练算法的性能产生了重大影响。要理解原因，回想一下，在普通的（非BatchNorm）深度神经网络中，损失函数不仅非凸，而且往往有大量“扭结”、平坦区域和尖锐极小值[17]。这使得基于梯度下降的训练算法不稳定（例如，由于梯度爆炸或消失），因此对学习率和初始化的选择高度敏感。

items.en: Now, the key implication of BatchNorm’s reparametrization is that it makes the gradients more reliable and predictive. After all, improved Lipschitzness of the gradients gives us confidence that when we take a larger step in a direction of a computed gradient, this gradient direction remains a fairly accurate estimate of the actual gradient direction after taking that step. It thus enables any (gradient–based) training algorithm to take larger steps without the danger of running into a sudden change of the loss landscape such as flat region (corresponding to vanishing gradient) or sharp local minimum (causing exploding gradients). This, in turn, enables us to use a broader range of (and thus larger) learning rates (see Figure 10 in Appendix B) and, in general, makes the training significantly faster and less sensitive to hyperparameter choices. (This also illustrates how the properties of BatchNorm that we discussed earlier can be viewed as a manifestation of this smoothening effect.)
items.zh: 现在，BatchNorm重参数化的关键含义是它使梯度更可靠和更具预测性。毕竟，梯度的Lipschitz性质改善让我们有信心，当我们沿着计算出的梯度方向迈出更大一步时，这个梯度方向在迈出那一步后仍然是实际梯度方向的相当准确的估计。因此，它使任何基于梯度的训练算法能够迈出更大的步长，而不会遇到损失景观的突然变化，如平坦区域（对应梯度消失）或尖锐局部极小值（导致梯度爆炸）。这反过来使我们能够使用更广泛（因此更大）的学习率（见附录B中的图10），并且通常使训练显著更快且对超参数选择不那么敏感。（这也说明了我们之前讨论的BatchNorm属性如何被视为这种平滑效果的表现。）

items.en: To demonstrate the impact of BatchNorm on the stability of the loss itself, i.e., its Lipschitzness, for each given step in the training process, we compute the gradient of the loss at that step and measure how the loss changes as we move in that direction – see Figure 4(a). We see that, in contrast to the case when BatchNorm is in use, the loss of a vanilla, i.e., non-BatchNorm, network has a very wide range of values along the direction of the gradient, especially in the initial phases of training. (In the later stages, the network is already close to convergence.)
items.zh: 为了展示BatchNorm对损失本身稳定性（即其Lipschitz性质）的影响，对于训练过程中的每个给定步骤，我们计算该步骤的损失梯度，并测量当我们沿该方向移动时损失如何变化——见图4(a)。我们看到，与使用BatchNorm的情况相比，普通（即非BatchNorm）网络的损失沿梯度方向的值范围非常宽，尤其是在训练的初始阶段。（在后期阶段，网络已经接近收敛。）

items.en: Similarly, to illustrate the increase in the stability and predictiveness of the gradients, we make analogous measurements for the ℓ2subscriptℓ2\ell_{2} distance between the loss gradient at a given point of the training and the gradients corresponding to different points along the original gradient direction. Figure 4(b) shows a significant difference (close to two orders of magnitude) in such gradient predictiveness between the vanilla and BatchNorm networks, especially early in training.
items.zh: 类似地，为了说明梯度稳定性和预测性的增加，我们对训练给定点的损失梯度与沿原始梯度方向不同点的梯度之间的ℓ2距离进行了类似测量。图4(b)显示了普通网络和BatchNorm网络在梯度预测性上的显著差异（接近两个数量级），尤其是在训练早期。

items.en: To further demonstrate the effect of BatchNorm on the stability/Lipschitzness of the gradients of the loss, we plot in Figure 4(c) the “effective” β𝛽\beta-smoothness of the vanilla and BatchNorm networks throughout the training. (“Effective” refers here to measuring the change of gradients as we move in the direction of the gradients.). Again, we observe consistent differences between these networks. We complement the above examination by considering linear deep networks: as shown in Figures 9 and 12 in Appendix B, the BatchNorm smoothening effect is present there as well.
items.zh: 为了进一步展示BatchNorm对损失梯度稳定性/Lipschitz性质的影响，我们在图4(c)中绘制了普通网络和BatchNorm网络在整个训练过程中的“有效”β-平滑度。（“有效”指的是当我们沿梯度方向移动时测量梯度的变化。）再次，我们观察到这些网络之间的一致差异。我们通过考虑线性深度网络来补充上述检验：如附录B中的图9和图12所示，BatchNorm平滑效果也存在。

items.en: Finally, we emphasize that even though our explorations were focused on the behavior of the loss along the gradient directions (as they are the crucial ones from the point of view of the training process), the loss behaves in a similar way when we examine other (random) directions too.
items.zh: 最后，我们强调，尽管我们的探索集中在损失沿梯度方向的行为（因为它们从训练过程的角度来看至关重要），但当我们检查其他（随机）方向时，损失也以类似方式表现。

full.sec: 3.3 Is BatchNorm the best (only?) way to smoothen the landscape?
full.secZh: 3.3 批归一化是平滑景观的最佳（唯一？）方式吗？

items.en: Given this newly acquired understanding of BatchNorm and the roots of its effectiveness, it is natural to wonder: Is this smoothening effect a unique feature of BatchNorm? Or could a similar effect be achieved using some other normalization schemes?
items.zh: 鉴于这种对BatchNorm及其有效性根源的新理解，自然会想：这种平滑效果是BatchNorm的独特特征吗？还是可以使用其他归一化方案实现类似效果？

items.en: To answer this question, we study a few natural data statistics-based normalization strategies. Specifically, we study schemes that fix the first order moment of the activations, as BatchNorm does, and then normalizes them by the average of their ℓpsubscriptℓ𝑝\ell_{p}-norm (before shifting the mean), for p=1,2,∞𝑝12p=1,2,\infty. Note that for these normalization schemes, the distributions of layer inputs are no longer Gaussian-like (see Figure 14). Hence, normalization with such ℓpsubscriptℓ𝑝\ell_{p}-norm does not guarantee anymore any control over the distribution moments nor distributional stability.
items.zh: 为了回答这个问题，我们研究了几种基于数据统计的自然归一化策略。具体来说，我们研究了固定激活的一阶矩（如BatchNorm所做）然后通过其ℓp范数的平均值（在移动均值之前）进行归一化的方案，其中p=1,2,∞。注意，对于这些归一化方案，层输入的分布不再像高斯分布（见图14）。因此，使用这种ℓp范数归一化不再保证对分布矩或分布稳定性的任何控制。

items.en: The results are presented in Figures 13, 12 and 12 in Appendix B. We observe that all the normalization strategies offer comparable performance to BatchNorm. In fact, for deep linear networks, ℓ1subscriptℓ1\ell_{1}–normalization performs even better than BatchNorm. Note that, qualitatively, the ℓpsubscriptℓ𝑝\ell_{p}–normalization techniques lead to larger distributional shift (as considered in [10]) than the vanilla, i.e., unnormalized, networks, yet they still yield improved optimization performance. Also, all these techniques result in an improved smoothness of the landscape that is similar to the effect of BatchNorm. (See Figures 12 and 12 of Appendix B.) This suggests that the positive impact of BatchNorm on training might be somewhat serendipitous. Therefore, it might be valuable to perform a principled exploration of the design space of normalization schemes as it can lead to better performance.
items.zh: 结果见附录B中的图13、12和12。我们观察到所有归一化策略都提供了与BatchNorm相当的性能。事实上，对于深度线性网络，ℓ1归一化甚至比BatchNorm表现更好。注意，定性上，ℓp归一化技术导致比普通（即未归一化）网络更大的分布偏移（如[10]中所考虑的），但它们仍然产生了改进的优化性能。此外，所有这些技术都导致了与BatchNorm效果类似的景观平滑度改善。（见附录B中的图12和12。）这表明BatchNorm对训练的积极影响可能有些偶然。因此，对归一化方案的设计空间进行原则性探索可能是有价值的，因为它可以带来更好的性能。

items.en: Our experiments so far suggest that BatchNorm has a fundamental effect on the optimization landscape. We now explore this phenomenon from a theoretical perspective. To this end, we consider an arbitrary linear layer in a DNN (we do not necessitate that the entire network be fully linear).
items.zh: 我们目前的实验表明BatchNorm对优化景观有根本性影响。我们现在从理论角度探索这一现象。为此，我们考虑DNN中的任意线性层（我们不需要整个网络完全线性）。

items.capEn: Figure 5: The two network architectures we compare in our theoretical analysis: (a) the vanilla DNN (no BatchNorm layer); (b) the same network as in (a) but with a BatchNorm layer inserted after the fully-connected layer W𝑊W. (All the layer parameters have exactly the same value in both networks.)
items.capZh: 图5：我们在理论分析中比较的两种网络架构：(a) 普通DNN（无BatchNorm层）；(b) 与(a)相同的网络，但在全连接层W之后插入了一个BatchNorm层。（两个网络中的所有层参数完全相同。）

items.en: We analyze the impact of adding a single BatchNorm layer after an arbitrary fully-connected layer W𝑊W at a given step during the training. Specifically, we compare the optimization landscape of the original training problem to the one that results from inserting the BatchNorm layer after the fully-connected layer – normalizing the output of this layer (see Figure 5). Our analysis therefore captures effects that stem from the reparametrization of the landscape and not merely from normalizing the inputs x𝑥x.
items.zh: 我们分析了在训练过程中某个给定步骤，在任意全连接层W之后添加单个BatchNorm层的影响。具体来说，我们将原始训练问题的优化景观与在全连接层之后插入BatchNorm层（归一化该层的输出）所产生的优化景观进行比较（见图5）。因此，我们的分析捕捉了源于景观重参数化的效果，而不仅仅是归一化输入x的效果。

items.en: We denote the layer weights (identical for both the standard and batch-normalized networks) as Wi​jsubscript𝑊𝑖𝑗W_{ij}. Both networks have the same arbitrary loss function ℒℒ\mathcal{L} that could potentially include a number of additional non-linear layers after the current one. We refer to the loss of the normalized network as ℒ^^ℒ\widehat{\mathcal{L}} for clarity. In both networks, we have input x𝑥x, and let y=W​x𝑦𝑊𝑥y=Wx. For networks with BatchNorm, we have an additional set of activations y^^𝑦\hat{y}, which are the “whitened” version of y𝑦y, i.e. standardized to mean 0 and variance 1. These are then multiplied by γ𝛾\gamma and added to β𝛽\beta to form z𝑧z. We assume β𝛽\beta and γ𝛾\gamma to be constants for our analysis. In terms of notation, we let σjsubscript𝜎𝑗\sigma_{j} denote the standard deviation (computed over the mini-batch) of a batch of outputs yj∈ℝmsubscript𝑦𝑗superscriptℝ𝑚y_{j}\in\mathbb{R}^{m}.
items.zh: 我们将层权重（标准网络和批归一化网络相同）记为Wij。两个网络都有相同的任意损失函数ℒ，该函数可能包含当前层之后的多个额外非线性层。为清晰起见，我们将归一化网络的损失记为ℒ̂。在两个网络中，我们有输入x，并令y=Wx。对于带有BatchNorm的网络，我们还有一组额外的激活ŷ，它们是y的“白化”版本，即标准化为均值0和方差1。然后这些乘以γ并加上β以形成z。我们假设β和γ在我们的分析中是常数。在符号方面，我们让σj表示一批输出yj∈ℝm的标准差（在小批量上计算）。

items.en: We begin by considering the optimization landscape with respect to the activations 𝒚𝒋subscript𝒚𝒋\bm{y_{j}}. We show that batch normalization causes this landscape to be more well-behaved, inducing favourable properties in Lipschitz-continuity, and predictability of the gradients. We then show that these improvements in the activation-space landscape translate to favorable worst-case bounds in the weight-space landscape.
items.zh: 我们首先考虑关于激活𝒚𝒋的优化景观。我们表明批归一化使该景观更良性，在Lipschitz连续性和梯度可预测性方面诱导出有利性质。然后我们表明激活空间景观的这些改进转化为权重空间景观中的有利最坏情况界限。

items.en: We first turn our attention to the gradient magnitude ‖∇𝒚𝒋ℒ‖normsubscript∇subscript𝒚𝒋ℒ\left|\left|{\nabla_{\bm{y_{j}}}\mathcal{L}}\right|\right|, which captures the Lipschitzness of the loss. The Lipschitz constant of the loss plays a crucial role in optimization, since it controls the amount by which the loss can change when taking a step (see [21] for details). Without any assumptions on the specific weights or the loss being used, we show that the batch-normalized landscape exhibits a better Lipschitz constant. Moreover, the Lipschitz constant is significantly reduced whenever the activations 𝒚^𝒋subscriptbold-^𝒚𝒋\bm{\hat{y}_{j}} correlate with the gradient ∇𝒚^jℒ^subscript∇subscriptbold-^𝒚𝑗^ℒ\nabla_{\bm{\hat{y}}_{j}}\widehat{\mathcal{L}} or the mean of the gradient deviates from 00. Note that this reduction is additive, and has effect even when the scaling of BN is identical to the original layer scaling (i.e. even when σj=γsubscript𝜎𝑗𝛾\sigma_{j}=\gamma).
items.zh: 我们首先关注梯度幅度‖∇𝒚𝒋ℒ‖，它捕捉了损失的Lipschitz性质。损失的Lipschitz常数在优化中起着关键作用，因为它控制着当迈出一步时损失可以改变的量（详见[21]）。在不假设特定权重或损失的情况下，我们表明批归一化景观表现出更好的Lipschitz常数。此外，每当激活𝒚̂𝒋与梯度∇𝒚̂_jℒ̂相关或梯度的均值偏离0时，Lipschitz常数显著减小。注意，这种减少是可加的，并且即使当BN的缩放与原始层缩放相同时（即即使当σj=γ时）也有效。

items.en: For a BatchNorm network with loss ℒ^^ℒ\widehat{\mathcal{L}} and an identical non-BN network with (identical) loss ℒℒ\mathcal{L},
items.zh: 对于一个损失为ℒ̂的BatchNorm网络和一个相同的损失为ℒ的非BN网络，

items.en: First, note that ⟨𝟏,∂L/∂y⟩2superscript1𝐿𝑦2\langle\bm{1},\partial L/\partial y\rangle^{2} grows quadratically in the dimension, so the middle term above is significant. Furthermore, the final inner product term is expected to be bounded away from zero, as the gradient with respect to a variable is rarely uncorrelated to the variable itself. In addition to the additive reduction, σjsubscript𝜎𝑗\sigma_{j} tends to be large in practice (cf. Appendix Figure 8), and thus the scaling by γσ𝛾𝜎\frac{\gamma}{\sigma} may contribute to the relative “flatness" we see in the effective Lipschitz constant.
items.zh: 首先，注意⟨𝟏,∂L/∂y⟩²随维度二次增长，因此中间项是显著的。此外，最终的内积项预计远离零，因为变量的梯度很少与变量本身不相关。除了可加性减少外，σj在实践中往往很大（参见附录图8），因此γ/σ的缩放可能有助于我们在有效Lipschitz常数中看到的相对“平坦性”。

items.en: We now turn our attention to the second-order properties of the landscape. We show that when a BatchNorm layer is added, the quadratic form of the loss Hessian with respect to the activations in the gradient direction, is both rescaled by the input variance (inducing resilience to mini-batch variance), and decreased by an additive factor (increasing smoothness). This term captures the second order term of the Taylor expansion of the gradient around the current point. Therefore, reducing this term implies that the first order term (the gradient) is more predictive.
items.zh: 我们现在关注景观的二阶性质。我们表明，当添加BatchNorm层时，损失Hessian关于激活在梯度方向上的二次形式，既被输入方差重新缩放（导致对小批量方差的鲁棒性），又被一个可加因子减小（增加平滑度）。该项捕捉了梯度在当前点附近的泰勒展开的二阶项。因此，减小该项意味着一阶项（梯度）更具预测性。

items.en: Let 𝐠^𝐣=∇𝐲𝐣ℒsubscriptbold-^𝐠𝐣subscript∇subscript𝐲𝐣ℒ\bm{\hat{g}_{j}}=\nabla_{\bm{y_{j}}}\mathcal{L} and 𝐇j​j=∂ℒ∂𝐲𝐣​∂𝐲𝐣subscript𝐇𝑗𝑗ℒsubscript𝐲𝐣subscript𝐲𝐣\bm{H}_{{jj}}=\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}}{\partial\bm{y_{j}}\partial\bm{y_{j}}} be the gradient and Hessian of the loss with respect to the layer outputs respectively. Then
items.zh: 令𝐠̂𝐣=∇𝐲𝐣ℒ和𝐇jj=∂ℒ/∂𝐲𝐣∂𝐲𝐣分别为关于层输出的损失梯度和Hessian。那么

items.en: If we also have that the 𝐇j​jsubscript𝐇𝑗𝑗\bm{H}_{{jj}} preserves the relative norms of 𝐠^𝐣subscriptbold-^𝐠𝐣\bm{\hat{g}_{j}} and ∇𝐲𝐣ℒ^subscript∇subscript𝐲𝐣^ℒ\nabla_{\bm{y_{j}}}\widehat{\mathcal{L}},
items.zh: 如果我们还有𝐇jj保持𝐠̂𝐣和∇𝐲𝐣ℒ̂的相对范数，

items.en: Note that if the quadratic forms involving the Hessian and the inner product ⟨𝒚^j,𝒈^𝒋⟩subscriptbold-^𝒚𝑗subscriptbold-^𝒈𝒋\left\langle{\bm{\hat{y}}_{j}},{\bm{\hat{g}_{j}}}\right\rangle are non-negative (both fairly mild assumptions), the theorem implies more predictive gradients. The Hessian is positive semi-definite (PSD) if the loss is locally convex which is true for the case of deep networks with piecewise-linear activation functions and a convex loss at the final layer (e.g. standard softmax cross-entropy loss or other common losses). The condition ⟨𝒚^j,𝒈^𝒋⟩>0subscriptbold-^𝒚𝑗subscriptbold-^𝒈𝒋0\left\langle{\bm{\hat{y}}_{j}},{\bm{\hat{g}_{j}}}\right\rangle>0 holds as long as the negative gradient 𝒈^𝒋subscriptbold-^𝒈𝒋\bm{\hat{g}_{j}} is pointing towards the minimum of the loss (w.r.t. normalized activations). Overall, as long as these two conditions hold, the steps taken by the BatchNorm network are more predictive than those of the standard network (similarly to what we observed experimentally).
items.zh: 注意，如果涉及Hessian矩阵的二次型和内积⟨𝒚^j,𝒈^𝒋⟩均为非负（两者都是相当温和的假设），则该定理意味着梯度更具预测性。当损失局部凸时，Hessian矩阵是半正定的，这对于具有分段线性激活函数和最终层凸损失（例如标准softmax交叉熵损失或其他常见损失）的深度网络成立。只要负梯度𝒈^𝒋指向损失（关于归一化激活）的最小值，条件⟨𝒚^j,𝒈^𝒋⟩>0就成立。总体而言，只要这两个条件成立，BatchNorm网络所采取的步骤就比标准网络更具预测性（与我们的实验观察类似）。

items.en: Note that our results stem from the reparametrization of the problem and not a simple scaling.
items.zh: 注意，我们的结果源于问题的重新参数化，而非简单的缩放。

items.en: For any input data X𝑋X and network configuration W𝑊W, there exists a BN configuration (W,γ,β)𝑊𝛾𝛽(W,\gamma,\beta) that results in the same activations yjsubscript𝑦𝑗y_{j}, and where γ=σj𝛾subscript𝜎𝑗\gamma=\sigma_{j}. Consequently, all of the minima of the normal landscape are preserved in the BN landscape.
items.zh: 对于任何输入数据X和网络配置W，存在一个BN配置(W,γ,β)，其产生相同的激活y_j，且γ=σ_j。因此，正常景观的所有最小值都保留在BN景观中。

items.en: Our theoretical analysis so far studied the optimization landscape of the loss w.r.t. the normalized activations. We will now translate these bounds to a favorable worst-case bound on the landscape with respect to layer weights. Note that a (near exact) analogue of this theorem for minimax gradient predictiveness appears in Theorem C.1 of Appendix C.
items.zh: 到目前为止，我们的理论分析研究了关于归一化激活的损失优化景观。现在，我们将这些界限转化为关于层权重的景观的有利最坏情况界限。注意，关于极小极大梯度预测性的该定理的（近乎精确）类似物出现在附录C的定理C.1中。

items.zh: 对于一个具有损失ℒ^的BatchNorm网络和一个相同的非BN网络（具有相同的损失ℒ），如果

items.en: Finally, in addition to a desirable landscape, we find that BN also offers an advantage in initialization:
items.zh: 最后，除了理想的景观外，我们发现BN在初始化方面也提供了优势：

items.en: Let 𝐖∗superscript𝐖\bm{W^{*}} and 𝐖^∗superscriptbold-^𝐖\bm{\widehat{W}^{*}} be the set of local optima for the weights in the normal and BN networks, respectively. For any initialization W0subscript𝑊0W_{0}
items.zh: 设𝐖∗和𝐖^∗分别为正常网络和BN网络中权重的局部最优集。对于任何初始化W_0

items.en: if ⟨W0,W∗⟩>0subscript𝑊0superscript𝑊0\left\langle{W_{0}},{W^{*}}\right\rangle>0, where W^∗superscript^𝑊\widehat{W}^{*} and W∗superscript𝑊W^{*} are closest optima for BN and standard network, respectively.
items.zh: 如果⟨W_0,W∗⟩>0，其中W^∗和W∗分别是BN和标准网络最近的最优点。

items.en: A number of normalization schemes have been proposed as alternatives to BatchNorm, including normalization over layers [1], subsets of the batch [31], or across image dimensions [30]. Weight Normalization [24] follows a complementary approach normalizing the weights instead of the activations. Finally, ELU [3] and SELU [13] are two proposed examples of non-linearities that have a progressively decaying slope instead of a sharp saturation and can be used as an alternative for BatchNorm. These techniques offer an improvement over standard training that is comparable to that of BatchNorm but do not attempt to explain BatchNorm’s success.
items.zh: 已经提出了许多归一化方案作为BatchNorm的替代方案，包括跨层归一化[1]、批次子集归一化[31]或跨图像维度归一化[30]。权重归一化[24]采用互补方法，归一化权重而非激活。最后，ELU[3]和SELU[13]是两个提出的非线性示例，它们具有逐渐衰减的斜率而非尖锐饱和，可用作BatchNorm的替代方案。这些技术提供了与BatchNorm相当的标准训练改进，但并未试图解释BatchNorm的成功。

items.en: Additionally, work on topics related to DNN optimization has uncovered a number of other BatchNorm benefits. Li et al. [9] observe that networks with BatchNorm tend to have optimization trajectories that rely less on the parameter initialization. Balduzzi et al. [2] observe that models without BatchNorm tend to suffer from small correlation between different gradient coordinates and/or unit activations. They report that this behavior is profound in deeper models and argue how it constitutes an obstacle to DNN optimization. Morcos et al. [19] focus on the generalization properties of DNN. They observe that the use of BatchNorm results in models that rely less on single directions in the activation space, which they find to be connected to the generalization properties of the model.
items.zh: 此外，与DNN优化相关主题的工作揭示了BatchNorm的其他一些好处。Li等人[9]观察到，使用BatchNorm的网络往往具有较少依赖参数初始化的优化轨迹。Balduzzi等人[2]观察到，没有BatchNorm的模型往往遭受不同梯度坐标和/或单元激活之间的小相关性。他们报告说，这种行为在更深层的模型中更为显著，并认为它构成了DNN优化的障碍。Morcos等人[19]关注DNN的泛化性质。他们观察到，使用BatchNorm导致模型较少依赖激活空间中的单一方向，他们发现这与模型的泛化性质相关。

items.en: Recent work [14] identifies simple, concrete settings where a variant of training with BatchNorm provably improves over standard training algorithms. The main idea is that decoupling the length and direction of the weights (as done in BatchNorm and Weight Normalization [24]) can be exploited to a large extent. By designing algorithms that optimize these parameters separately, with (different) adaptive step sizes, one can achieve significantly faster convergence rates for these problems.
items.zh: 最近的工作[14]确定了简单、具体的设置，其中使用BatchNorm训练的变体可证明优于标准训练算法。主要思想是，解耦权重的长度和方向（如BatchNorm和权重归一化[24]中所做）可以在很大程度上被利用。通过设计分别优化这些参数并采用（不同的）自适应步长的算法，可以针对这些问题实现显著更快的收敛速度。

items.en: In this work, we have investigated the roots of BatchNorm’s effectiveness as a technique for training deep neural networks. We find that the widely believed connection between the performance of BatchNorm and the internal covariate shift is tenuous, at best. In particular, we demonstrate that existence of internal covariate shift, at least when viewed from the – generally adopted – distributional stability perspective, is not a good predictor of training performance. Also, we show that, from an optimization viewpoint, BatchNorm might not be even reducing that shift.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们研究了BatchNorm作为训练深度神经网络技术有效性的根源。我们发现，广泛认为的BatchNorm性能与内部协变量偏移之间的联系充其量是脆弱的。特别是，我们证明，至少从普遍采用的分布稳定性角度来看，内部协变量偏移的存在并不是训练性能的良好预测指标。此外，我们表明，从优化角度来看，BatchNorm甚至可能并未减少这种偏移。

items.en: Instead, we identify a key effect that BatchNorm has on the training process: it reparametrizes the underlying optimization problem to make it more stable (in the sense of loss Lipschitzness) and smooth (in the sense of “effective” β𝛽\beta-smoothness of the loss). This implies that the gradients used in training are more predictive and well-behaved, which enables faster and more effective optimization. This phenomena also explains and subsumes some of the other previously observed benefits of BatchNorm, such as robustness to hyperparameter setting and avoiding gradient explosion/vanishing. We also show that this smoothing effect is not unique to BatchNorm. In fact, several other natural normalization strategies have similar impact and result in a comparable performance gain.
items.zh: 相反，我们确定了BatchNorm对训练过程的一个关键影响：它重新参数化底层优化问题，使其更稳定（在损失Lipschitz连续性意义上）且更平滑（在损失“有效”β-平滑性意义上）。这意味着训练中使用的梯度更具预测性和良好行为，从而实现更快、更有效的优化。这一现象也解释并包含了先前观察到的BatchNorm的其他一些好处，例如对超参数设置的鲁棒性和避免梯度爆炸/消失。我们还表明，这种平滑效应并非BatchNorm独有。事实上，其他几种自然归一化策略也有类似影响，并产生可比的性能提升。

items.en: We believe that these findings not only challenge the conventional wisdom about BatchNorm but also bring us closer to a better understanding of this technique. We also view these results as an opportunity to encourage the community to pursue a more systematic investigation of the algorithmic toolkit of deep learning and the underpinnings of its effectiveness.
items.zh: 我们相信，这些发现不仅挑战了关于BatchNorm的传统观点，而且使我们更接近更好地理解这一技术。我们还将这些结果视为一个机会，鼓励社区对深度学习算法工具包及其有效性的基础进行更系统的研究。

items.en: Finally, our focus here was on the impact of BatchNorm on training but our findings might also shed some light on the BatchNorm’s tendency to improve generalization. Specifically, it could be the case that the smoothening effect of BatchNorm’s reparametrization encourages the training process to converge to more flat minima. Such minima are believed to facilitate better generalization [8, 11]. We hope that future work will investigate this intriguing possibility.
items.zh: 最后，我们在此关注的是BatchNorm对训练的影响，但我们的发现也可能揭示BatchNorm倾向于改善泛化的原因。具体来说，可能是BatchNorm重新参数化的平滑效应鼓励训练过程收敛到更平坦的最小值。这些最小值被认为有助于更好的泛化[8, 11]。我们希望未来的工作将研究这一有趣的可能性。

items.en: We thank Ali Rahimi and Ben Recht for helpful comments on a preliminary version of this paper.
items.zh: 我们感谢Ali Rahimi和Ben Recht对本文初稿的有益评论。

items.en: Shibani Santurkar was supported by the National Science Foundation (NSF) under grants IIS-1447786, IIS-1607189, and CCF-1563880, and the Intel Corporation. Dimitris Tsipras was supported in part by the NSF grant CCF-1553428 and the NSF Frontier grant CNS-1413920. Andrew Ilyas was supported in part by NSF awards CCF-1617730 and IIS-1741137, a Simons Investigator Award, a Google Faculty Research Award, and an MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab research grant. Aleksander Mądry was supported in part by an Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship, a Google Research Award, and the NSF grants CCF-1553428 and CNS-1815221.
items.zh: Shibani Santurkar得到国家科学基金会（NSF）资助（IIS-1447786、IIS-1607189和CCF-1563880）以及英特尔公司的支持。Dimitris Tsipras部分得到NSF资助CCF-1553428和NSF前沿资助CNS-1413920的支持。Andrew Ilyas部分得到NSF奖项CCF-1617730和IIS-1741137、Simons研究员奖、Google教师研究奖以及MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab研究资助的支持。Aleksander Mądry部分得到Alfred P. Sloan研究奖学金、Google研究奖以及NSF资助CCF-1553428和CNS-1815221的支持。
